notes: Public-source carbon model for cloudprice-mcp v0.10. PUE comes from each cloud's published sustainability report; grid carbon intensity (gCO2e/kWh) uses location-based published averages for the region the catalog tracks. Renewable matching reflects each cloud's public claim for the relevant year. Market-based residual = grid_intensity * (1 - renewable_match_pct/100).

sources.pue_method: Each provider's most recent published global or region PUE (Sustainability Report / ESG disclosure).
sources.grid_intensity_method: Region-specific gCO2e/kWh from public grid-emissions data (EPA eGRID 2023 for US, similar published baselines elsewhere). Values are annual averages.
sources.renewable_matching_method: Provider's published annual renewable match. AWS/Azure claim 100% as of 2024/2025; GCP publishes CFE (carbon-free-energy) percentages per region; OCI claims 100% in EU but doesn't publish per-region figures, so we use a conservative 0% match for non-EU.
sources.power_model_method: Estimated CPU+memory power based on industry-published per-core figures for the relevant CPU family. ARM (Graviton, Ampere, Axion) ~30% lower than x86. Memory ~0.3W/GB across all families. Assumes 50% average utilization.

power_model._notes: Final watts = (vcpus * watts_per_vcpu + memory_gb * memory_watts_per_gb) * utilization_factor. PUE multiplier applied separately at total-power stage.
